summary: Lead started M6-DIPLOMACY-LEGITIMACY-001 after M6 Alpha system contract closure entered main. simulation_engineer writer thread 019f0616-e69a-7be1-a688-ed29590a37f1 is assigned to implement the deterministic diplomacy and legitimacy substrate within the task allowed paths.

tests_run.result: M6-DIPLOMACY-LEGITIMACY-001 and M6-SCENARIO-DATA-PIPELINE-001 were dependency-ready on main before start.
Spawned simulation_engineer writer thread 019f0616-e69a-7be1-a688-ed29590a37f1, nickname Delta the 2nd.

decisions: Start M6-DIPLOMACY-LEGITIMACY-001 as the highest-priority dependency-ready M6 task after M6 Alpha contract closure entered main.
Preserve DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE and do not implement manual node battle, server/multiplayer, telemetry, arbitrary-code scripting, full peace treaty UI, or hidden grand-strategy manager.
Do not modify PROJECT_STATUS.md because this task lists it as a forbidden path.
risks: This R4 task touches shared simulation/protocol/save surfaces; systems_architect review is required before acceptance.
Protocol or save-format changes beyond additive schemas may require ADR if they change a frozen contract.
Untracked ui-samples/ exists and is unrelated, unstaged, and untouched.
requested_action: Implement the deterministic diplomacy and legitimacy substrate within allowed paths, run required tests, and return a simulation_engineer REVIEW handoff for systems_architect review.

acceptance_criteria: Diplomacy and legitimacy are represented in authoritative sim-core state with stable branded IDs, explicit schema versioning, reason-coded events, and deterministic tie-breakers.
Player and AI diplomacy actions use the same GameCommand paths and produce serializable rejection reasons without UI-only mutation or hidden AI privileges.
Legitimacy affects start-to-victory decisions, obligations, succession pressure, war/postwar consequences, and AI reasoning without becoming a generic global manager.
Save/load and replay preserve diplomacy and legitimacy state and reject malformed, missing, or future-version payloads safely.
required_tests: Property/invariant tests cover command parity, stable ordering, no diplomacy graph cycles, legitimacy conservation/reason codes, and save rejection paths.
